message:
numpy: add tensordot python wrapper + beads sweep

Adds a PyO3 wrapper for numpy.tensordot to fnp-python, matching NumPy's
signature (a, b, axes=None with default 2). The implementation is a
pure passthrough to np.tensordot. The `axes` argument accepts both
call shapes NumPy supports: an integer N (sum over the last N axes of
a against the first N of b) or a 2-tuple of per-side axis sequences
(explicit per-side contraction indices). Both forms forward verbatim;
when None is passed we materialize 2_i64 to preserve NumPy's default.

Registered in the fnp_python module table adjacent to vdot and cross.

Beads sweep: include in the numpy python wrapper parity queue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
